title: dichaetanthera asperrima
intro: Dichaetanthera asperrima, also known as the Rough Bitterwood or Katsuriki in Japanese, is a species of flowering plant in the family Theaceae. Traditionally, Dichaetanthera asperrima has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant's health benefits are attributed to its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Dichaetanthera asperrima can be prepared into various herbal preparations, including tea infusions, infusions for baths, and even crude extracts for topical applications.
